message: Jake
Kristy
Günter
Oh, long time no see. I was thinking of taking the day off from work.
That's
My apologies.
Being rude is just the usual, I'm used to it by now.
Is it you again?
Look
Talk
Listen
Think
Move
System
The state of the area around
The serial murder case
The matter of
The matter of
The matter of
The serial murder case

The walls and floor are uniformly dark, and the shelves are filled with what appear to be medical books. The only decoration is a green potted plant by the window.
A sterile, utterly utilitarian office devoid of any frills.
In a sense,
there's no room more suited for this.
It's an extremely dreary and gloomy room.
Can anyone truly relax and work in such a place?
In his early thirties, medium build, with a slightly receding hairline. Is it due to stress from work?
Wearing a very traditional suit as usual, complete with a tie. If the shirt were white, he could pass for someone at a funeral.
A condescending attitude, common among doctors and lawyers.
I don't think I could grow to like her much.
Since you intend to take time off work, why are you here? It’s lucky for me, though.
I came to pick up some materials for the conference.
I see, so that’s what it was.
What on earth were you imagining?
Do you have some worries or something? You don’t look well.
Well, not exactly worries, just various things going on.
Hmm, well if something comes up, feel free to come for counseling anytime.
It seems you have a negative impression of psychiatrists and counseling. Is it because of Miss Dern?
No.
It’s not like that though.
The risk of being afflicted by madness exists for everyone, you know. Seeking counseling before it happens is not a waste.
I'll think about it.
Come for counseling anytime. I'll give you a discount.
Excuse me, but,
do you not know the travel destination of
Miss Dern?
Oh, is Miss Dern traveling?
Ah, you didn't know.
She hasn’t been coming for counseling anymore. The last time I saw her, she seemed fine when her fiancé returned.
To be honest, I don't believe her mental state has become healthy, but as long as things are going well with her fiancé, it should be fine.
I see.
So, she’s still not completely better.
Even if there can be improvements, full recovery is quite difficult.
As long as she's getting along with her fiancé, I think she’ll be okay.
By the way, you said before that
it wouldn't be surprising if someone like her committed murder.
Did I say such a thing? Well, I did think that the risk is higher for her compared to the average person.
For instance, would it be particularly true if it were related to her fiancé?
Uh-huh.
Well, I had thought it was past trauma, but the probability of killing someone related to one's fiancée is quite high.
For example, there’s a rift between the fiancée and oneself.
If you investigate the cause, there might be a new woman involved.
Yes.
In that case, I think Miss Dern would have a significant chance of wanting to eliminate the rival.
Is that so?
Do you have something in mind?
N-No.
Well, what I’m saying isn’t a definitive fact but merely a discussion of possibilities, so no need to worry.
Mental illness can be quite troublesome, you see.
What do you think about the serial murder case?
Are you referring to some of the recent murders happening in New York?
Yes, that's right. What do you think about the perpetrator's strange behavior of dismembering victims?
Could it be the work of a pervert, or is there some purpose behind it?
I can't be precise until I meet the perpetrator.
The topic of this murder case comes up among patients too. What purpose does this murderer have in cutting up and taking away bodies?
That’s the problem. Would someone go that far out of spite?
I feel this crime isn't motivated by such simple hatred.
Modern people's minds are quite sick; it’s a serious issue.
It seems they don’t believe I’ve fully recovered. I also can’t understand her drastic change since Guy returned.
It's been a while since we've met.
Is the doctor out of touch with social matters, or does he not know about my detention as a suspect?
Then again, it wasn’t widely reported, so I guess it’d be odd if he did know.
It seems like as a psychiatrist, she isn't interested in the culprit. I guess her patients are all that matter to her?
I wonder
if he's truly a murderer?
She said she wouldn't go to counseling,
so she probably wasn't telling the doctor where she was headed.
Right now,
her mind is occupied with suspicion.
Rather, it’s more accurate to say it’s about Guy and
her.
In New York, the number of victims who were dismembered has already reached four.
The serial murder case that started in Los Angeles had one victim at a time in New York.
Yet here in New York, there are four victims.
Something feels off. Or is it just that the culprit has changed their style?
The criminal is a pretty smart guy.
I can’t believe that a bunch of cops could arrest him.
